tldr: Despite a lumpy 163 km opener, the sprinters got the last word: Christophe Laporte (Team Visma | Lease a Bike) won the reduced bunch kick in Pizarra to take the first leader's jersey, beating Bastien Tronchon and Ben Oliver.

narrative.headline: Laporte powers to the first leader's jersey
narrative.body: Victor Campenaerts lit up the start with a solo move, cresting the first climb (Puerto del Madroño) over a minute clear before sitting up to wait for the bunch. A second move — Luca Creti, Ryan Boulahoite, Josh Burnett, Ibai Azanza, Nicolás Alustiza, Dylan Vandenstorme and Lucas Towers — was caught on the final climb, the Puerto de las Abejas, where a reduced peloton kept all the big names aboard. The lumpy parcours never fully shed the fast men, and the run-in came down to a reduced bunch sprint. Laporte timed his effort perfectly to beat Bastien Tronchon and Ben Oliver, claiming the win and the first overall lead of the race.

gcImpact.headline: Laporte into the leader's jersey
gcImpact.body: With the top of the stage finishing on the same time, Laporte led the GC ahead of Tronchon, Ben Oliver, Søren Wærenskjold and Fred Wright — all on the same time, the race wide open with the time bonuses on stage 1 the only separator.

sources.title: Ruta del Sol 2026: Laporte powers to first leader's jersey
